title: Revisiting Professional Forecasters’ Past Performance and the Outlook for 2026
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Professional forecasters predict 1.9% GDP growth for 2026, with significant variance among predictions. The consensus outlook is lower than the top forecasts, w
excerpt: Professional forecasters predict 1.9% GDP growth for 2026, with significant variance among predictions. The consensus outlook is lower than the top forecasts, which average 2.5%. The outlook for inflation is expected to be 2.9%.

content.introduction: Professional forecasters predict 1.9% GDP growth for 2026, with significant variance among predictions. The consensus outlook is lower than the top forecasts, which average 2.5%. The outlook for inflation is expected to be 2.9%.
body: # Professional Forecasters’ Past Performance and the 2026 Economic Outlook. Image 2: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis. Image 3 FRED Tools and resources to find and use economic data worldwide. Image 4 FRASER U.S. Financial, economic, and banking history. Image 5 ALFRED Vintages of economic data from specific dates in history. Image 7: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis mobile eagle logo. About the St. Louis Fed. *   Image 9: In Focus: News and Notes from the St. Louis Fed. Subscribe to our In Focus newsletter. *   Fed & U.S. Economic History. U.S. Financial, economic, and banking history. # Revisiting Professional Forecasters’ Past Performance and the Outlook for 2026. The consensus outlook for real GDP growth in 2026 is 1.9%, but it seems that forecasters have considerable disagreement on the outlook for the year ahead, as reflected in the following:. *   The average of the top 10 GDP growth rate forecasts is 2.5%, while the average of the bottom 10 forecasts is 1.2%.
For 2026, professional forecasters expect 1.9% GDP growth and a 2.9% increase in the consumer price index. Suggested Citation. Charles S. Gascon, 2025. "
Abstract: So far, the 2025 forecasts have turned out to be rather accurate. For 2026, professional forecasters expect 1.9% GDP growth and a 2.9%
